notes: Data sourced from GitHub Advanced Security via GitHub CLI (gh)
Secret scanning returned 0 alerts; may reflect no secrets detected or permission limitations
CodeQL alerts include intentionally planted vulnerabilities (OWASP Juice Shop is a training target)
Dependabot ecosystem dates represent advisory publication dates, not package ecosystem
All findings are SUPLEMENTAL CANDIDATES requiring validation against actual vulnerability context

alerts.title: Dangerous file processing in fileUpload.ts
alerts.description: Zip extraction, XML entity expansion, and YAML parsing are dangerous, but the most dangerous processing is guarded by challenge-enabled checks that need deployment confirmation.
alerts.validation_note: Intentional training vulnerability - verify no unintended exposure

alerts.title: MarsDB $where server-side JavaScript in trackOrder.ts
alerts.description: The code uses MarsDB $where server-side JavaScript, and npm audit reports marsdb command injection, but exploitability is partly gated by challenge/config sanitization branches.
alerts.validation_note: Intentional training vulnerability - verify no unintended exposure

alerts.title: JWT cookies set without security flags
alerts.description: JWT cookies are set without HttpOnly, Secure, or SameSite flags while CORS is globally permissive and state-changing cookie-authenticated endpoints exist.
alerts.validation_note: Intentional training vulnerability - verify no unintended exposure

alerts.title: Open redirect via substring allowlist
alerts.description: Redirect allowlist checks substrings instead of exact origin/path matching, allowing attacker-controlled domains containing an allowed URL string.
alerts.validation_note: Intentional training vulnerability - verify no unintended exposure

alerts.title: Arbitrary field selection in whoami endpoint
alerts.description: The whoami endpoint lets a client select arbitrary fields from the authenticated user object, which is populated from SELECT * during login and includes sensitive properties such as password hash and TOTP secret.
alerts.validation_note: Intentional training vulnerability - verify no unintended exposure

alerts.title: Passwords stored as unsalted MD5 hashes
alerts.description: Passwords are stored as unsalted MD5 hashes, making offline cracking fast after any database disclosure.
alerts.validation_note: Intentional training vulnerability - verify no unintended exposure

alerts.title: Payment card numbers stored without encryption
alerts.description: Full payment card numbers are stored as application data without tokenization or encryption at rest; masking only occurs at response time.
alerts.validation_note: Intentional training vulnerability - verify no unintended exposure

alerts.title: SSRF in profile image URL upload
alerts.description: Authenticated users can cause server-side fetches to arbitrary URLs with no scheme, host, DNS, IP-range, or response-size restrictions.
alerts.validation_note: Intentional training vulnerability - verify no unintended exposure

alerts.title: Insecure basket access control
alerts.description: Authenticated basket read and checkout fetch baskets solely by URL id and do not constrain by the authenticated user's UserId/basket id.
alerts.validation_note: Intentional training vulnerability - verify no unintended exposure

alerts.title: Mass assignment in user registration
alerts.description: Public user registration is handled by a generated resource that can mass-assign the role field; the model permits admin/accounting values.
alerts.validation_note: Intentional training vulnerability - verify no unintended exposure

alerts.title: Sensitive files publicly accessible
alerts.description: Sensitive operational files, order PDFs, logs, key directory, API docs, and metrics are publicly routed without authentication or network ACLs.
alerts.validation_note: Intentional training vulnerability - verify no unintended exposure

alerts.title: Hardcoded JWT private key
alerts.description: JWT private key is hardcoded in source and key files are served over HTTP; authorization trusts JWT claims including role.
alerts.validation_note: Intentional training vulnerability - verify no unintended exposure

alerts.title: SQL injection in search endpoint
alerts.description: Public unauthenticated search parameter is placed inside raw SQL and can be used for UNION/data-exfiltration attacks.
alerts.validation_note: Intentional training vulnerability - verify no unintended exposure

alerts.title: SQL injection in login endpoint
alerts.description: Unauthenticated login input is interpolation directly into SQL before authentication, enabling authentication bypass and user row disclosure.
alerts.validation_note: Intentional training vulnerability - verify no unintended exposure

alerts.title: YAML upload parsing vulnerability (fixed)
alerts.description: YAML upload parses untrusted YAML with js-yaml load

alerts.title: sanitize-html version vulnerability (fixed)
alerts.description: Reachable obsolete sanitize-html package may allow XSS sanitizer bypasses

alerts.title: Public metrics endpoint (fixed)
alerts.description: Public unauthenticated metrics endpoint exposes operational telemetry

alerts.title: JWT cookie security flags (fixed)
alerts.description: JWT cookie is set without explicit HttpOnly, Secure, or SameSite flags

alerts.title: Vulnerable express-jwt usage (fixed)
alerts.description: Vulnerable express-jwt is used for route authorization

alerts.title: Missing CSRF protection (fixed)
alerts.description: Cookie-authenticated state changes lack CSRF protection

alerts.title: Whoami field disclosure (fixed)
alerts.description: Whoami field selection can disclose sensitive JWT user properties

alerts.title: Open redirect vulnerability (fixed)
alerts.description: Open redirect via substring allowlist

alerts.title: Payment card storage (fixed)
alerts.description: Payment card numbers are stored as plaintext application data

alerts.title: Weak password hashing (fixed)
alerts.description: Passwords are hashed with unsalted MD5

alerts.title: Template injection (fixed)
alerts.description: Template rendering accepts user-controlled layout data

alerts.title: SSRF in profile image upload (fixed)
alerts.description: Authenticated SSRF through profile image URL fetch

alerts.title: Zip Slip vulnerability (fixed)
alerts.description: Zip Slip archive extraction can write outside upload directory

alerts.title: XXE vulnerability (fixed)
alerts.description: XML upload enables XXE file disclosure

alerts.title: NoSQL injection in order tracking (fixed)
alerts.description: NoSQL injection in unauthenticated order tracking

alerts.title: NoSQL injection in product reviews (fixed)
alerts.description: NoSQL server-side JavaScript injection in product reviews

alerts.title: file-type affected by infinite loop in ASF parser on malformed input with zero-size sub-header
alerts.description: A denial of service vulnerability exists in the ASF (WMV/WMA) file type detection parser. When parsing a crafted input where an ASF sub-header has a `size` field of zero, the parser enters an infinite loop. Any application that uses `file-type` to detect the type of untrusted/attacker-controlled input is affected. An attacker can stall the Node.js event loop with a 55-byte payload.

alerts.title: Multer Vulnerable to Denial of Service via Uncontrolled Recursion
alerts.description: A vulnerability in Multer versions < 2.1.1 allows an attacker to trigger a Denial of Service (DoS) by sending malformed requests, potentially causing stack overflow.

alerts.title: Multer vulnerable to Denial of Service via incomplete cleanup
alerts.description: A vulnerability in Multer versions < 2.1.0 allows an attacker to trigger a Denial of Service (DoS) by sending malformed requests, potentially causing resource exhaustion.

alerts.title: Multer vulnerable to Denial of Service via resource exhaustion
alerts.description: A vulnerability in Multer versions < 2.1.0 allows an attacker to trigger a Denial of Service (DoS) by dropping connection during file upload, potentially causing resource exhaustion.

alerts.title: sanitize-html is vulnerable to XSS through incomprehensive sanitization
alerts.description: sanitize-html prior to version 2.0.0-beta is vulnerable to Cross-site Scripting (XSS). The sanitizeHtml() function does not sanitize content when using the custom transformTags option.

alerts.title: Multer vulnerable to Denial of Service via unhandled exception from malformed request
alerts.description: A vulnerability in Multer versions >= 1.4.4-lts.1, < 2.0.2 allows an attacker to trigger a Denial of Service (DoS) by sending a malformed request. This request causes an unhandled exception, leading to a crash of the process.

alerts.title: Multer vulnerable to Denial of Service via unhandled exception
alerts.description: A vulnerability in Multer versions >=1.4.4-lts.1, <2.0.1 allows an attacker to trigger a Denial of Service (DoS) by sending an upload file request with an empty string field name. This request causes an unhandled exception, leading to a crash of the process.

alerts.title: Multer vulnerable to Denial of Service from maliciously crafted requests
alerts.description: A vulnerability in Multer versions >=1.4.4-lts.1 allows an attacker to trigger a Denial of Service (DoS) by sending a malformed multi-part upload request. This request causes an unhandled exception, leading to a crash of the process.

alerts.title: Multer vulnerable to Denial of Service via memory leaks from unclosed streams
alerts.description: Multer <2.0.0 is vulnerable to a resource exhaustion and memory leak issue due to improper stream handling. When the HTTP request stream emits an error, the internal busboy stream is not closed, violating Node.js stream safety guidance.

alerts.title: socket.io has an unhandled 'error' event
alerts.description: A specially crafted Socket.IO packet can trigger an uncaught exception on the Socket.IO server, thus killing the Node.js process.

alerts.title: Sanitize-html Vulnerable To REDoS Attacks
alerts.description: The package sanitize-html before 2.7.1 are vulnerable to Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) due to insecure global regular expression replacement logic of HTML comment removal.

alerts.title: sanitize-html Information Exposure vulnerability
alerts.description: Versions of the package sanitize-html before 2.12.1 are vulnerable to Information Exposure when used on the backend and with the style attribute allowed, allowing enumeration of files in the system.

alerts.title: jsonwebtoken vulnerable to signature validation bypass due to insecure default algorithm in jwt.verify()
alerts.description: In versions <=8.5.1 of jsonwebtoken library, lack of algorithm definition and a falsy secret or key in the jwt.verify() function can lead to signature validation bypass due to defaulting to the none algorithm for signature verification.

alerts.title: jsonwebtoken's insecure implementation of key retrieval function could lead to Forgeable Public/Private Tokens from RSA to HMAC
alerts.description: Versions <=8.5.1 of jsonwebtoken library can be misconfigured so that passing a poorly implemented key retrieval function will result in incorrect verification of tokens. Tokens signed with an asymmetric public key could be verified with a symmetric HS256 algorithm.

alerts.title: jsonwebtoken unrestricted key type could lead to legacy keys usage
alerts.description: Versions <=8.5.1 of jsonwebtoken library could be misconfigured so that legacy, insecure key types are used for signature verification. For example, DSA keys could be used with the RS256 algorithm.

alerts.title: Improper Input Validation in sanitize-html
alerts.description: Apostrophe Technologies sanitize-html before 2.3.1 does not properly handle internationalized domain name (IDN) which could allow an attacker to bypass hostname whitelist validation set by the allowedIframeHostnames option.

alerts.title: Improper Input Validation in sanitize-html
alerts.description: Apostrophe Technologies sanitize-html before 2.3.2 does not properly validate the hostnames set by the allowedIframeHostnames option when the allowIframeRelativeUrls is set to true, which allows attackers to bypass hostname whitelist for iframe element.

alerts.title: Command Injection in marsdb
alerts.description: All versions of marsdb are vulnerable to Command Injection. In the DocumentMatcher class, selectors on $where clauses are passed to a Function constructor unsanitized. This allows attackers to run arbitrary commands in the system when the function is executed.

alerts.title: Authorization bypass in express-jwt
alerts.description: Versions before and including 5.3.3, we are not enforcing the algorithms entry to be specified in the configuration. When algorithms is not specified in the configuration, with the combination of jwks-rsa, it may lead to authorization bypass.

alerts.title: Cross-Site Scripting in sanitize-html
alerts.description: Affected versions of sanitize-html do not sanitize input recursively, which may allow an attacker to execute arbitrary Javascript.

alerts.title: Cross-Site Scripting in sanitize-html
alerts.description: Affected versions of sanitize-html are vulnerable to cross-site scripting when allowedTags includes at least one nonTextTag.

alerts.title: Verification Bypass in jsonwebtoken
alerts.description: Versions 4.2.1 and earlier of jsonwebtoken are affected by a verification bypass vulnerability. This is a result of weak validation of the JWT algorithm type, occurring when an attacker is allowed to arbitrarily specify the JWT algorithm.
